errors.message: outcomes[1] ('branch-guarded-thaw').triggeredBy lists a choice combination that is structurally impossible given the scene graph. choice-2b:a routes to scene-4-pairing-warm, whose defaultNextScene is scene-5-library-warm — a scene gated by choice-3 (not choice-3b). Therefore 'choice-3b:a' can never co-occur with 'choice-2b:a' in an actual playthrough.
errors.expectedValue: A choice sequence reachable via a single playthrough path (e.g. choice-1:b, choice-2b:a, choice-3:a, choice-4:? )
errors.fix: Correct the triggeredBy list to only include choices from a single reachable path, or change the branch-matching logic to use partial/weighted trait scoring instead of an exact literal choice-history match.

errors.message: outcomes[2] ('branch-popularity-cost').triggeredBy lists a choice combination that is structurally impossible. choice-2:b routes to scene-4-pairing-cold → scene-5-library-cold, which is gated by choice-3b (not choice-3). 'choice-3:b' cannot follow 'choice-2:b'.
errors.expectedValue: A reachable sequence, e.g. ["choice-1:a","choice-2:b","choice-3b:b","choice-4b:b"] (or similar valid path)
errors.fix: Replace 'choice-3:b' with the correct choice id from the reachable branch (choice-3b), and re-verify the full path resolves to a real end scene consistent with the described outcome.

errors.message: Two dialogue lines use an undocumented 'text2' field in addition to 'text', which does not appear anywhere else in the dialogue schema. Rendering pipelines that only read 'text' will silently drop this content, breaking the line ('—three facts and a grade level.' and part of the raccoon-court joke).
errors.location: scenes[0].dialogue[12] (id: s1-13) and choiceDialogue (choiceId: choice-3b response for scene-5-library-cold, dialogue id: s5c-4)
errors.expectedValue: A single 'text' string containing the full line, or an 'action'/'text' split consistent with the rest of the episode
errors.actualValue: {"action":"gestures at the cards","text2":"—three facts and a grade level."} / similar in s1-13
errors.fix: Merge text2 content into the 'text' field (or into 'action' if it's meant as a stage direction) so no dialogue content is lost during rendering.

warnings.message: The four outcome branches are derived from an exact literal sequence of choice ids across differently-numbered choice steps (choice-3 vs choice-3b, choice-4 vs choice-4b vs choice-4c). Because different playthrough paths pass through structurally different choice ids at the 'same' narrative beat, any future edits to the branching tree are likely to reintroduce impossible triggeredBy combinations (as found in this review). Consider normalizing choice ids per beat (e.g., always 'choice-3' with contextual scene binding) or documenting the valid path set per outcome explicitly.
warnings.suggestion: Add a validation step that simulates each outcome's triggeredBy list against the actual scene/choice graph to confirm reachability before marking DRAFT content as reviewable.

warnings.message: scene-6-showdown-solid, scene-6-showdown-strained, and scene-6-showdown-fractured list 'jordan' and 'mr-alvarez' as present characters, but neither speaks nor is referenced in the dialogue or narration for those scenes.
warnings.suggestion: Either give Jordan/Alvarez a small reactive beat (even non-verbal, via NARRATOR) or remove them from the characters list if they're not meaningfully present in that beat.

warnings.message: INTERNAL dialogue lines are used extensively as an implicit internal-monologue channel; this is consistent throughout but relies entirely on convention rather than schema enforcement.
warnings.suggestion: No action needed for this episode, but confirm the rendering pipeline has explicit, tested support for 'INTERNAL' as a stylized non-dialogue channel distinct from NARRATOR.

recommendations: Run an automated reachability/consistency check that walks every choice path from scene-1 to an END transition and cross-validates the resulting choice-history against each outcome's triggeredBy list.
Standardize the dialogue line schema (id, character, text, action, emotion) and lint against stray fields like 'text2' before merging content.
Consider explicitly documenting, per outcome, one canonical example playthrough (the exact scene sequence) alongside the triggeredBy choice list, to make the intended path auditable at a glance.
